seoTitle: How to Download and Install Trello on PC
metaDescription: Install the Trello desktop app on Windows through the Microsoft Store, sign in with your Atlassian account, and set up desktop notifications.
intro: Running Trello as a dedicated desktop app instead of a browser tab can cut down on distractions and keep you from missing notifications. This guide covers downloading Trello for Windows through the Microsoft Store and getting it configured.

steps.heading: Open the Microsoft Store
steps.body: Launch the Microsoft Store on your Windows PC and search for the official Trello app.

steps.heading: Verify the listing
steps.body: Confirm you're installing the verified, free release published by Atlassian.

steps.heading: Install the app
steps.body: Click Install and let the automatic background installation complete.

steps.heading: Sign in with your Atlassian account
steps.body: Open the app and log in using your existing Atlassian single sign-on credentials.

steps.heading: Enable desktop notifications
steps.body: Turn on native operating system alert badges so you can monitor card updates and mentions without manually refreshing.

steps.heading: Set up custom shortcuts
steps.body: Explore custom global desktop shortcuts to quickly spawn new task cards from anywhere on your PC.

faq.question: Is the Trello desktop app free to download?
faq.answer: Yes, the Windows app is available for free through the Microsoft Store.

faq.question: Do I need a separate account for the desktop app?
faq.answer: No, you can sign in with your existing Atlassian account credentials through single sign-on.

faq.question: What's the benefit of using the desktop app over the browser?
faq.answer: It runs independently of your browser tabs and supports native OS notifications and custom keyboard shortcuts.

faq.question: Will I still get notified about card mentions?
faq.answer: Yes, once you activate native alert badges, you'll see updates without needing to manually refresh a browser tab.
